Mastery-Based Education Pilot Program

IC 20-20-54 (HEA 1515, 2025) — This program supports Indiana schools in shifting from traditional seat-time-based progression to competency-based advancement.

📋 IC 20-20-54 📅 Expires June 30, 2035 🏫 School Corps & Charters
ℹ
Program Overview
Read before completing your application
📖
Statutory Definitions (IC 20-20-54)
Mastery (Sec. 1): Evidenced attainment of predefined, rigorous learning objectives aligned to Indiana Academic Standards.
Mastery-Based Education (Sec. 2): An innovative, learner-centered approach focusing on mastery of specific skills or knowledge areas rather than time spent in a classroom.
📅
Pilot Timeline
Planning Phase (Fall 2026 – Spring 2027) → Full Pilot Launch (Fall 2027–2028) → Evaluation & Reporting → Ongoing through Summer 2028–29
⚠️
Cannot be waived: Collective bargaining rights · Protections for students with disabilities · Civil rights protections · ESSA requirements · State standardized assessments*
* State standardized assessments are permissible to waive under IC 20-20-54 Sec. 8; however, IDOE will not consider waiver requests for state standardized assessments during this round of the pilot.
✅
Smart Form Features: This application auto-fills known information from the Indiana school directory when you type your school or corporation name. All auto-filled fields are editable.

Section 1 of 12
2
Pilot Scope & Structure
Define how your school will participate
🏫 Single School Site

A school corporation applying to implement the pilot in one specific school within its corporation. The corporation is the applicant; the pilot is scoped to a single designated school site.

🏢 School Corporation

A school corporation applying to implement the pilot across two or more of its schools. The corporation is the applicant and designates which schools within the corporation will participate.

🤝 Consortium of Schools

Two or more separate school corporations and/or charter schools submitting a joint application. One must serve as the Lead Entity. Section 3 will be required.

Section 2 of 12
3
Consortium Application
Complete only if applying as a consortium of schools
ℹ️
A consortium allows two or more school corporations and/or charter schools to submit a joint application. One school corporation or charter must be designated as the Lead Entity for fiscal and administrative purposes.
3A — Lead Entity
Managing Partner — Primary Contact
Managing Partner — Secondary Contact
3B — Consortium Member Schools

6
Leadership & Staffing
IC 20-20-54 requires strong local leadership for sustained implementation
📋
Note: Detailed governance protocols and replacement procedures will be developed during the Planning Year (Fall 2026). The MBL Leadership Team must be formally established by end of Fall 2026.

Section 9 of 12
10
Anticipated Resource Needs
Estimated costs your school or corporation expects to incur during the pilot
📋
IDOE is committed to supporting participating schools throughout the MBE Pilot and is actively exploring funding opportunities to help offset implementation costs. While mastery-based education is fundamentally a shift in practice rather than a driver of new expenditure, we recognize that navigating the realities of a pilot program — including uncertainty, iteration, and responding to lessons learned — may give rise to one-time or short-term costs along the way. To inform our planning, please describe any additional resources your school or corporation anticipates needing across each phase of the pilot. This information will help IDOE prioritize and align available supports. Any funding made available through the pilot will be communicated to approved applicants during the Planning Year.
Year 1 2026–27 Planning Phase
Year 2 2027–28 Full Pilot Launch
Year 3 2028–29 Evaluation
Section 10 of 12
11
Requested Regulatory Waivers
IC 20-20-54 Sec. 8 — only if applicable
⚠️
Cannot be waived under any circumstances: Collective bargaining rights · Protections for students with disabilities · Civil rights protections · ESSA requirements
*
State Standardized Assessments: While IC 20-20-54 Sec. 8 permits the suspension of certain statutes — including those governing state standardized assessments — IDOE will not consider waiver requests for state standardized assessments during this round of the pilot. Please do not include assessment waivers in your request.
Waiver Requests
Indiana Code / IAC Provision Why It Is a Barrier to MBL Proposed Alternative Approach Intended Impact
Section 11 of 12
12
Certification & Authorized Signature
Read all certifications carefully before signing

By signing below, the authorized signatory certifies that:

  • ✓ All information provided in this application is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge.
  • ✓ The applicant understands that participation in the MBE Pilot Program is contingent upon IDOE approval and compliance with IC 20-20-54 and all applicable Indiana Code provisions.
  • ✓ The applicant agrees to submit data and reports as requested by IDOE under IC 20-20-54 Sec. 7.
  • ✓ The applicant acknowledges that IDOE may rescind approval if the pilot is not implemented in accordance with the approved plan.
  • ✓ Participation is voluntary and the applicant may withdraw with appropriate notice to IDOE.
📬
